text:
BOTTOM LINE — Risk-off, semis dumped, comms and banks bid. Broad indices masked a violent factor rotation as semiconductors and AI hardware unwound while Communication Services and Financials were bought on higher-rate expectations and still-greedy sentiment (Fear & Greed 67.5). Large over small by 0.24pp plus rising dollar and falling long bonds point to a tactically defensive tilt rather than full de-risking.

INDICES — VIX 16.6, drifting lower versus its 20d MA at 18.1. SPY -0.14%, RSP +0.30%, DIA 0.00%, IWM -0.38%, QQQ -1.52%, SOXX -6.41%; rank: RSP > DIA > SPY > IWM > QQQ > SOXX. STYLE: LARGE > SMALL by 0.24pp. Most notable cross-asset move: USO -2.98% with TLT -1.04% as long rates back up.  

VOLUME — SPY vol_ratio 0.95x and QQQ vol_ratio 0.95x show average index activity. Elevated RSP 1.4x and IWM 1.0x versus soft DIA 0.8x signal attention on breadth and small caps. Sector volume was heaviest in XLC 2.3x and XLU 1.5x with XLK light at 0.7x, so comms buying looks like accumulation while tech selling had surprisingly muted volume.

SECTORS & ROTATION — XLC Communication Services +2.44%, XLF Financials +2.18%, XLY Discretionary +0.69%, XLV Healthcare +0.55%, XLB Materials +0.37%, XLRE Real Estate +0.34%, XLP Staples +0.28%, XLE Energy -0.56%, XLI Industrials -1.01%, XLU Utilities -1.26%, XLK Technology -2.57%. Top industries: IGV Software +3.02%, KWEB China Internet +2.66%; bottom: SMH Semis -5.40%, DRAM Memory -10.82%. Money in: comms, banks, software; money out: semis, crypto-linked WGMI -6.82% and DRAM. Tape message: AI hardware fading / high-to-low rotation.

POSITIONING — SPY PCR 0.93 with skew +4.0% shows moderate protection demand but not panic. QQQ PCR 1.01 and skew +9.9% flag concentrated Nasdaq downside hedging tied to the semi flush. IWM PCR 1.75 with skew +6.2% and spot still above its 304.0 gamma flip level point to dealers dampening moves rather than amplifying. VIX below its 20d MA confirms complacent vol despite today’s factor stress.

CROSS-ASSET — Dollar UUP +0.28% and gold GLD +0.60% rose together while long Treasuries TLT -1.04% and oil USO -2.98% fell, a classic higher-real-rate, geopolitical-cooldown mix. The move fits a macro-neutral backdrop with energy risk premium fading even as inflation prints keep rates elevated.

SIGNALS & WATCH — Fear & Greed 67.5 (Greed), top movers: META +8.81% (New Staples 7), KLAC -11.77% (Fab 7), WGMI -6.82% (Industries), with 20d momentum extremes in SOXX +4.86% ytd +91.18% and XLE -7.84% 20d. META’s 2.6x vol_ratio with price +8.81% screams aggressive accumulation in megacap comms, while KLAC’s 2.9x vol_ratio and -11.77% print is textbook distribution in semis. KLAC -11.77% and TER -?% are both hit in a broader semi repricing tied to fears of AI capex overshoot and crowded positioning. Watch: META +8.81% for continuation and potential leadership shift; KBE +1.70% on 3.3x volume for financials break higher; IJT -0.76% on 12.8x volume for possible small-cap factor inflection near its style regime BEAR reading.
